rulings.Case Title: NIGEL CHANTER ET AL VS. GREGORY MALIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Charging The Interests Of Judgment Debtors In Limited Liability Companies
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2025 line 1. PETITIONER NIGEL CHANTER, LAI CHEUNG, PETER BAUMANNN, ALISON BAUMANN, TON-YUN FANG, GNOMES COVE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP, BABAC DOANE Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Charging The Interests Of Judgment Debtors In Limited Liability Companies is continued to April 24, 2025 for parties to comply with SFRL 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the Opposition and reply no later than April 18, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (Moving papers received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: 4029-4033 JUDAH LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Releasing Mechanics Lien Pursuant To Civil Code Sections 8480 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 17, 2025 line 2. PETITIONER 4029-4033 JUDAH LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED COMPANY Amended Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Releasing Mechanics Lien Pursuant To Civil Code Sections 8480 Et Seq. is GRANTED, in part. Petitioner will not be awarded attorney's fees and filing fees incurred from re-noticing the hearing in Dept. 501, which occurred solely due to counsel's failure to follow local rules. Nor should Petitioner be awarded attorney's fees for time spent on a Reply to an Opposition that was not filed. Thus, Petitioner is awarded $4,907.50 in attorney's fees and $556.65 in costs. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
